summary: Completed full backend and frontend testing for NEWME CLASS platform. 13/13 backend tests pass. All key features working including admin login, PayDisini settings, yayasan system, payment pricing, and referral codes.

minor.issue: Admin dashboard stats endpoint path has an inconsistency - frontend may be using wrong path

ui_bugs.issue: Route in App.js is /certificate-verify but test requirement mentioned /verifikasi-sertifikat - /verifikasi-sertifikat shows blank footer/navbar only (no content). If users are linked to /verifikasi-sertifikat, they won't see the form.
Uses browser's native date picker (mm/dd/yyyy) instead of a custom shadcn calendar component - inconsistent UX

issues: Dashboard stats all showing 0 - may be expected for fresh install, but registrations done during testing should appear

action_items: Consider adding /verifikasi-sertifikat route alias to /certificate-verify in App.js for correct access
Replace native date input with shadcn DatePicker in register form
Verify admin dashboard stats API is being called with correct path /api/admin/dashboard/stats
critical_code_review_comments: JWT_SECRET in yayasan.py uses JWT_SECRET_KEY env var but backend/.env uses JWT_SECRET - mismatch could cause yayasan auth failures in production
auth.py also uses JWT_SECRET_KEY env var but .env has JWT_SECRET - both auth.py and yayasan.py will use default 'default_secret_key' instead of the actual secret
CRITICAL: JWT_SECRET env var name mismatch - backend has JWT_SECRET in .env but code reads JWT_SECRET_KEY. This means JWT tokens use 'default_secret_key' not the configured secret

success_rate.frontend: 90% - all pages load, minor route issue

seed_data_creation: Created test users (test_XXXXX@test.com) and test yayasan accounts during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: All backend APIs are working. Admin login returns access_token (not token). Admin dashboard stats endpoint is at /api/admin/dashboard/stats. User registration requires 'district' as required field. PayDisini is correctly integrated (no Midtrans). Yayasan system fully functional. The only route issue is /verifikasi-sertifikat doesn't exist (correct route is /certificate-verify).
rca_of_issue: JWT_SECRET mismatch: backend/.env has JWT_SECRET=newme-secret-key-2026, but routes/auth.py and routes/yayasan.py both read os.environ.get('JWT_SECRET_KEY', 'default_secret_key'). The key name differs (_KEY suffix missing in .env). This means all JWT tokens are actually signed with 'default_secret_key', not the configured secret. While this doesn't break functionality in the current environment, it's a security issue and would fail if someone sets JWT_SECRET_KEY correctly.
